notes: Source is the ManualsLib hardware installation manual (primary) supplemented by networktigers.com and networkdevicesinc.com product pages for power supply connector detail. The model has two variants: -E (Network Essentials) and -A (Network Advantage); ports are identical. Power input is IEC 320-C16 (high-temperature variant of C14 used on the PWR-C5-1KWAC supply); mapped to 'iec' as the closest allowed enum. USB console is USB mini-Type B (5-pin), mapped to 'usb-micro' as closest allowed enum. StackWise rear stacking connectors are proprietary and omitted (no matching connector type). A second optional hot-swap power supply slot exists on the rear but ships empty; only the populated AC In port is listed. SFP uplink speed confirmed as 1G (4x 1G fixed) per model designation "4G" in SKU.

ports.name: Console (RJ-45)
ports.note: RJ-45 console port; supplied with RJ-45-to-DB-9 cable

ports.name: Console (USB)
ports.note: USB mini-Type B (5-pin) console port; connector is mini-B not micro-B — closest available enum is usb-micro

ports.name: USB Storage
ports.note: USB Type-A port for flash storage (supports up to 8 GB Cisco USB flash drives)

ports.name: AC In
ports.note: Power supply module PWR-C5-1KWAC; actual inlet is IEC 320-C16 (high-temp C14 variant / 'kettle' notch) — closest available enum is iec (C13/C14); 100–240 VAC, 1000 W
